rag.description: The SaveScreenshot action (aliases: GetScreenshot, TakeScreenshot) captures and saves a PNG screenshot to disk using the W3C WebDriver GET /session/{session id}/screenshot command. It supports full-page viewport captures and element-level screenshots when OnElement and Locator are supplied. The target directory is created automatically via Directory.CreateDirectory including any missing parent directories. The file name defaults to a new GUID and always receives a .png extension if not already present (case-insensitive). If a locator is supplied but GetElement returns null the plugin falls back to a full-page screenshot unconditionally. Every saved absolute path is appended to the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots session parameter, accumulating across all invocations in the session.

qa.question: What does the SaveScreenshot plugin do?
qa.answer: It captures a PNG screenshot of either the full browser viewport or a specific element and saves it to disk using the W3C WebDriver screenshot command. The output directory is created automatically if absent, and every saved absolute path is appended to the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots session parameter. The action is also known by aliases GetScreenshot and TakeScreenshot.

qa.question: What parameters does SaveScreenshot accept?
qa.answer: It accepts two optional parameters: Directory (the folder to save the screenshot into, defaulting to Environment.CurrentDirectory) and FileName (the file name, defaulting to a new GUID string with .png appended automatically when the name does not already end with .png).

qa.question: How does SaveScreenshot choose between a full-page and an element screenshot?
qa.answer: When OnElement and Locator are provided the plugin calls this.GetElement(rule, element) to resolve the target. If that returns a non-null element it calls element.SaveScreenshot to scope the capture. If GetElement returns null — either because OnElement was absent or the element was not found — the plugin calls WebDriver.SaveScreenshot to capture the full browser viewport. The fallback is unconditional and silent; no error is raised.

qa.question: What is the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots output parameter?
qa.answer: SaveScreenshot:Screenshots is a session parameter holding a JSON array of absolute file paths for every screenshot saved during the session. Each invocation deserializes the existing list, appends the new path, and re-serializes it, so the parameter accumulates paths across multiple calls rather than replacing them.

qa.question: How is the file name determined and what extension is enforced?
qa.answer: The FileName parameter value is used when supplied. If omitted a new GUID string is generated as the default name. In both cases the plugin appends .png if the name does not already end with .png (case-insensitive check via String.EndsWith), ensuring every saved file is a valid PNG regardless of what the caller provides.

qa.question: What happens when the specified Directory does not exist?
qa.answer: The plugin calls Directory.CreateDirectory before writing the file. This creates the full directory path including any missing parent directories automatically. No error is raised for a new or previously absent directory.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for SaveScreenshot?
What aliases are available for SaveScreenshot?
qa.answer: SaveScreenshot is also accepted as GetScreenshot and TakeScreenshot — all three resolve to identical behavior.

description: ### Purpose

Captures and saves a PNG screenshot of the browser viewport or a specific element during automation workflows, using the W3C WebDriver `GET /session/{session id}/screenshot` command as its underlying mechanism.

It automatically creates the target directory, enforces the `.png` extension on every output file, generates a unique GUID-based file name when none is provided, and accumulates every saved absolute path in the `SaveScreenshot:Screenshots` session parameter for downstream use.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                 | Description                                                                                                                                                    |
| Full-Page Screenshot    | Captures the entire browser viewport as a PNG file when no target element is specified.                                                                        |
| Element Screenshot      | Captures only the specified element when OnElement and Locator are supplied, using the element-level screenshot call.                                          |
| Null-Element Fallback   | Falls back to a full-page screenshot when a locator is supplied but GetElement returns null, making the action unconditionally safe to invoke.                 |
| Auto Directory Creation | Calls Directory.CreateDirectory before writing, creating the target directory and any missing parent directories automatically.                                |
| Auto File Naming        | Generates a unique GUID-based file name when FileName is omitted, preventing accidental overwrites across repeated invocations.                               |
| PNG Enforcement         | Always appends .png to the output file name if it does not already end with .png (case-insensitive), ensuring every saved file is a valid PNG.                |
| Session Output          | Appends each saved absolute path to the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots session parameter, accumulating a complete path list across all invocations in the session. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case              | Description                                                                                                                               |
| Audit Trail           | Capture screenshots at key workflow steps to create a visual record for compliance review and process documentation.                      |
| Error Documentation   | Take a screenshot when an unexpected state is detected to provide visual evidence that pinpoints the exact failure point for analysis.     |
| Step Evidence Capture | Save a screenshot after a critical form submission or navigation to confirm the application transitioned to the expected state.            |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                   | Description                                                                                                                                            |
| Visual Regression Testing  | Capture screenshots at fixed checkpoints across test runs and compare the accumulated SaveScreenshot:Screenshots paths to detect unintended UI changes. |
| Cross-Browser Verification | Save viewport and element screenshots across browser configurations to confirm consistent rendering and layout behaviour.                              |
| Failure Evidence           | Collect a screenshot immediately at the point of assertion failure to provide a reproducible visual artifact for root-cause analysis.                  |

edge_cases: Directory does not exist — Directory.CreateDirectory is called first and creates it automatically
FileName already ends with .png — no second extension is appended; the name is used as-is
FileName does not end with .png — .png is appended before writing the file
Directory path contains spaces or special characters — Path.Combine handles them; the caller is responsible for otherwise valid path characters
File with the same name already exists in the directory — the existing file is silently overwritten
Session parameter SaveScreenshot:Screenshots is not yet initialised — the plugin defaults to an empty JSON array and starts a new accumulation list
annotations.expected_result: A file named PageScreenshot.png is saved under the Screenshots directory. Its absolute path is appended to the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots session parameter.
annotations.notes: Both Directory and FileName are optional. When both are supplied the plugin uses them directly, appending .png to FileName only if not already present.

description: ### Full-page screenshot with custom directory and file name
Captures a screenshot of the full browser viewport and saves it as `PageScreenshot.png` in the `Screenshots` directory.
The saved absolute path is appended to the `SaveScreenshot:Screenshots` session parameter.

edge_cases: No argument supplied — Directory defaults to Environment.CurrentDirectory and FileName defaults to a new GUID string
Current working directory is read-only — Directory.CreateDirectory does not throw for an existing directory, but the file write fails with UnauthorizedAccessException
Multiple calls in the same session — each generates a unique GUID-named file; all paths accumulate in SaveScreenshot:Screenshots without collision
Session already contains paths in SaveScreenshot:Screenshots — the new path is appended to the existing JSON array, not replacing it
WebDriver session is unexpectedly terminated — the screenshot call throws a WebDriverException before the file is written
annotations.expected_result: A PNG file with a GUID-based name is saved in the current working directory. Its absolute path is appended to the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots session parameter.
annotations.notes: When the plugin is invoked with no argument both Directory and FileName use their defaults. Repeated calls safely accumulate paths in the session parameter.

description: ### Full-page screenshot with default settings
Captures a screenshot of the full browser viewport using all default settings.
This is the simplest invocation — no directory or file name configuration is required.

edge_cases: Element is not found by the locator — GetElement returns null; the plugin falls back to a full-page screenshot
Element is present in the DOM but not visible — the element screenshot may capture an empty or transparent region
Element is partially outside the viewport — the screenshot captures only the visible portion of the element
Directory does not exist — created automatically before the file is written
FileName already ends with .png — used as-is without appending a second extension
Multiple element screenshots in the same session with different FileName values — each path is appended to SaveScreenshot:Screenshots without collision
annotations.expected_result: A screenshot of the element identified by #ClickButton is saved as ElementScreenshot.png in the Screenshots directory. Its absolute path is appended to the SaveScreenshot:Screenshots session parameter.
annotations.notes: When OnElement and Locator are both provided the plugin calls element.SaveScreenshot, scoping the capture to that element only. If the element cannot be found the plugin silently falls back to a full-page screenshot.

description: ### Element-level screenshot with CSS selector
Captures a screenshot scoped to the element identified by the CSS selector `#ClickButton` and saves it as `ElementScreenshot.png` in the `Screenshots` directory.
When `OnElement` and `Locator` are provided the plugin calls `element.SaveScreenshot` instead of the full-page capture.
If the element is not found by the locator the plugin falls back to a full-page screenshot.
The saved absolute path is appended to the `SaveScreenshot:Screenshots` session parameter.
A JSON array of absolute file paths for every screenshot saved during the automation session.
Each invocation of SaveScreenshot appends the new path to the existing list, so the parameter accumulates paths across multiple calls in the same session.
Use this parameter in downstream steps to validate, move, rename, or attach the saved screenshots.
outputParameters.name: SaveScreenshot:Screenshots

description: The directory where the screenshot file is saved.
Defaults to the current working directory when omitted.
parameters.name: Directory

description: The name of the screenshot file.
When omitted a new GUID string is used as the file name.
parameters.name: FileName

description: The locator strategy used to find the element specified in OnElement.
When OnElement is absent this property is ignored and the plugin takes a full-page screenshot.
properties.name: Locator

description: A locator expression that identifies the element to screenshot.
When supplied the plugin captures only that element instead of the full browser viewport.
When the element cannot be found the plugin falls back to a full-page screenshot without raising an error.
properties.name: OnElement

summary: SaveScreenshot (also accepted as GetScreenshot and TakeScreenshot) captures and saves a PNG screenshot of the full browser viewport or a specific element to disk during automation workflows.

It automatically creates the target directory, enforces the `.png` extension on every output file, generates a unique GUID-based file name when none is provided, and appends every saved absolute path to the `SaveScreenshot:Screenshots` session parameter for downstream consumption.
Use this action whenever visual evidence, regression baselines, element-level captures, or screenshot audit trails are required in RPA workflows or automated test suites.
